title: US Treasuries Are Uninvestible
text:
US Treasuries Are Uninvestible

Charles Gave

Gavekal Research | 25 Jun 2026

In recent years, I have maintained that the government bond markets of the United States, France and the United Kingdom have been uninvestible (see Stable Financial Systems Versus Unstable Financial Systems and The Euthanasia Of The Rentier). The time has come to reassess that view. While the discussion in this paper will focus on the US bond market, the conclusions are broadly similar for the UK and France.Is this the first time in the last half century that the US bond market has been uninvestible for more than a decade? Hardly. A look back to the 1970s reveals a decade marked by a seemingly never-ending succession of economic catastrophes in the US and beyond.
